title: L'accord des adjectifs
lesson: In French, the adjective agrees in gender (masculine/feminine) and number (singular/plural) with the noun. Regular case: grand, grande, grands, grandes. You add -e for the feminine and -s for the plural. Common irregular adjectives: beau/belle, nouveau/nouvelle, blanc/blanche, heureux/heureuse, vieux/vieille. In the plural: beaux, nouveaux, blancs/blanches, heureux (invariable in the masculine plural), vieux.

items.explanation: Maison is feminine singular, so grand → grande (you add -e).
Livres is masculine plural, so intéressant → intéressants (you add -s).
Voitures is feminine plural: petit → petite → petites (feminine -e + plural -s).
Beau is irregular: its feminine form is belle. Robe is feminine singular.
Nouveau is irregular: its feminine form is nouvelle. Voiture is feminine singular.
Blanc is irregular: its feminine form is blanche. Chemise is feminine singular.
Adjectives ending in -eux form their feminine in -euse: heureux → heureuse.

blanks.hint: irregular adjective, invariable in the masculine plural

items.explanation: Vieux is invariable in the masculine plural: vieux. (In the feminine: vieille / vieilles.)
In the masculine plural, beau becomes beaux (adjectives ending in -eau take -x).
Fleurs is feminine plural: blanc → blanche → blanches.

items.text: Mon frère est plus {1} que moi.
items.explanation: Frère is masculine singular: the adjective stays in its base form, grand.
Heureux in the feminine singular is heureuse, and in the feminine plural heureuses.
